UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and top-level settings, including basic identification and system interaction data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

role.description: The user's designated role within the application (e.g., 'user', 'admin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp of the user's most recent activity or login.

defaultModeId.description: Reference to the ID of the mode (e.g., 'MinimalMode', 'FocusMode') that the user prefers as their default. (Relationship: Mode 1:N UserProfile)

UserPreference.title: UserPreference
UserPreference.description: Stores detailed user interface and functional preferences, including visual profile settings and schema versioning for migration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPreference entity. For a single preferences document per user, this might be a fixed identifier like 'main'.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that these preferences belong to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserPreference)

schemaVersion.description: Version number of the preference schema, crucial for managing updates and data migrations without breaking existing user configurations.

visualProfileDensity.description: Setting for the UI density (e.g., 'compact', 'comfortable'), impacting spacing and element size.

visualProfileMotion.description: Preference for UI animations and transitions (e.g., 'reduced' for minimal motion, 'full' for standard animations).

visualProfileContrast.description: Setting for the UI's visual contrast (e.g., 'normal', 'high').

visualProfileFontScaling.description: Factor by which the base font size is scaled in the UI (e.g., 1.0 for default, 1.2 for larger text).

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when these user preferences were last successfully migrated to a newer schema version.

UserLayout.title: UserLayout
UserLayout.description: Defines a customizable dashboard layout configuration for a user, including the arrangement and specific settings of individual panels.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLayout entity (e.g., 'my-market-layout', used in 'users/{uid}/layouts/{layoutId}').

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that this layout belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserLayout)

schemaVersion.description: Version number of the layout schema, used to ensure compatibility during application updates and data migrations.

modeId.description: The identifier of the display mode (e.g., 'MinimalMode', 'ProDeskMode') this layout is designed for or associated with.

layoutName.description: A user-friendly name or title for this specific dashboard layout (e.g., 'My Main Chart Layout').

panelConfigJsonStrings.description: An array where each string is a JSON representation of a panel's configuration, containing its stable ID, type, order, and specific settings.

gridConfigRows.description: The total number of rows defined in the grid system for this layout.

gridConfigCols.description: The total number of columns defined in the grid system for this layout.

gridConfigLayoutJsonStrings.description: An array where each string is a JSON representation of a panel's position and size within the grid (including 'i', 'x', 'y', 'w', 'h' properties).

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this layout was last successfully migrated to a newer schema version.

UserEvent.title: UserEvent
UserEvent.description: Records significant user interaction events within the application, such as onboarding milestones and mode usage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserEvent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that generated this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserEvent)

eventType.description: The specific type of event that occurred (e.g., 'onboarding_started', 'mode_selected', 'panel_added').

eventDataJsonString.description: A JSON string representing a flexible object containing additional context or data relevant to the specific event (e.g., {'modeId': 'MinimalMode'}).

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the event occurred.

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.description: Captures structured user feedback, bug reports, issues, or 'confusion points' reported by users for continuous improvement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feedback entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted this feedback. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Feedback)

description.description: A detailed textual description of the feedback, issue, or suggestion.

screenshotUrl.description: An optional URL pointing to a screenshot or image that visually supports the feedback.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the feedback was submitted.

currentPage.description: The URL or identifier of the page/view where the user was when they submitted the feedback.

modeId.description: Optional ID of the mode the user was actively using when the feedback was submitted.

layoutId.description: Optional ID of the dashboard layout the user was viewing when the feedback was submitted.

ExperimentAssignment.title: ExperimentAssignment
ExperimentAssignment.description: Records a user's assignment to a specific variant within an A/B test experiment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExperimentAssignment entity, typically the same as the userId (Firebase Auth UID), indicating a 1:1 relationship.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who has been assigned to this experiment variant. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 ExperimentAssignment)

experimentName.description: The name or identifier of the A/B experiment (e.g., 'onboarding_variant', 'default_mode_ordering_test').

variant.description: The specific variant ('control', 'variantA', 'workflow_copy') that the user has been assigned to for this experiment.

assignedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user was assigned to this particular experiment variant.

definition.description: Stores user profiles and top-level settings. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. Includes the user's `role` for database-based access control (DBAC).
Stores detailed user interface and functional preferences. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Often `preferenceId` will be a fixed string like 'main'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: preferenceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific set of user preferences (e.g., 'main').

definition.description: Defines customizable dashboard layout configurations for a user. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized fields like `schemaVersion` and `modeId` for versioning and context.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: layoutId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user-defined dashboard layout.

definition.description: Records significant user interaction events within the application. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence, allowing users to create their own events. Events are typically immutable after creation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user event.

definition.description: Captures structured user feedback. Includes denormalized `userId` field for authorization independence during creation and for specific read rules. Primarily intended for user creation and admin review.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific feedback submission.

definition.description: Records a user's assignment to an A/B test variant. The document ID `{userId}` is the `request.auth.uid`, ensuring direct lookup and authorization independence for each user's assignment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, serving as the document ID and corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. Central to this is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** and **Structural Segregation**.

All user-specific data (UserProfile, UserPreference, UserLayout, UserEvent) is rooted under a dedicated `/users/{userId}` collection. This path-based ownership is the primary mechanism for authorization. For example, for any document in `/users/{userId}/preferences/{preferenceId}`, the security rule `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` immediately establishes ownership without requiring any `get()` operations to parent documents. The `userId` wildcard in the path acts as an implicit denormalization of the owner's ID, thereby eliminating hierarchical authorization dependencies and enabling atomic operations.

**Structural Segregation** is maintained by ensuring that all documents within a given collection or subcollection share a homogeneous security posture. For instance, `/users/{userId}/preferences` is strictly for a single user's preferences, and `/users/{userId}/layouts` for that same user's layouts. This avoids complex conditional logic in security rules and improves clarity.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** are directly supported by this structure. For any subcollection under `/users/{userId}`, a `list` operation initiated by an authenticated user will automatically be scoped to their `userId` in the path, ensuring they can only list their own data securely and efficiently. For instance, `db.collection('users').doc(uid).collection('layouts').get()` will only return `layout` documents belonging to `uid`, validated by the path itself.

For `Feedback` and `ExperimentAssignment` which are top-level collections, explicit denormalization of the `userId` field within each document is used. For `/feedback/{feedbackId}`, `allow create: if request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId;` enforces that the creator's UID matches the `userId` field. Reading/listing for `Feedback` is primarily intended for administrators, allowing for `allow read, list: if get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'admin';` (assuming the role is stored on the UserProfile). If users need to read their own feedback, the `read` rule would check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`, and any `list` operation would explicitly need to include a `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` filter which the rule could enforce (`allow list: if request.auth.uid != null && request.query.where.userId == request.auth.uid;`).

For `/experimentAssignments/{userId}`, the `userId` is used as the document ID directly. This allows a user to `get('/experimentAssignments/$(request.auth.uid)')` their own assignment with a simple `allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId;` rule, ensuring direct authorization independence. This design makes the intent of access control explicit and significantly reduces the complexity of Firestore security rules, making the application launch-safe and highly debuggable.
